findings: Destination Port feature exhibits a dominant value with a ratio of 1.0 for DDoS and 0.346 for BENIGN, indicating strong class-conditioned distribution differences.
High Jensen-Shannon divergence (0.7315) confirms significant distributional separation between classes on this feature.
Dependency contextualization did not reveal mitigating structural diversity, suggesting over-reliance risk on this feature's dominant port values in classification models.
limitations: Analysis limited to local evidence from e33; broader dataset context or model internals not examined.
